title: Encyclia × gracilis
intro: Encyclia × gracilis, also known as the gracilis orchid, is a hybrid plant species within the Encyclia genus, characterized by its unique and delicate flowers. The health benefits of Encyclia × gracilis include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of certain infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Encyclia × gracilis is commonly prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, as well as being used in the production of tinctures and capsules, to harness its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Encyclia × gracilis is a hybrid orchid species, and its classification is as follows: it belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is a combination of two Encyclia species. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a hybrid of Encyclia cordigera and Encyclia flava. This classification helps in identifying and differentiating the species from other orchids. Further classification may be provided at the genus or species level, but the hybrid nature of Encyclia × gracilis is widely accepted. The classification of Encyclia × gracilis plays a significant role in understanding its characteristics and behavior.
